question_text: A student rubs a balloon on their hair and it becomes negatively charged. Which statement best explains why the balloon attracts small pieces of paper?

options.label: A
options.text: The paper becomes positively charged and is attracted to the negative balloon

options.label: B
options.text: The negative charge on the balloon induces a positive charge on the near side of the paper, causing attraction

options.label: C
options.text: The paper is repelled by the balloon's negative charge

options.label: D
options.text: The balloon transfers electrons to the paper, making them both positively charged

explanation: When a negatively charged object comes near a neutral conductor like paper, the negative charge on the balloon repels electrons in the paper away from the near side. This leaves a net positive charge on the side closest to the balloon, causing electrostatic attraction. The paper remains overall neutral but has an induced charge distribution.
